connectorUiConfig.title: Mulesoft CloudHub Connector (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Mulesoft CloudHub data connector enables you to ingest alerts from Mulesoft CloudHub into Microsoft Sentinel through the CloudHub REST API v2. This connector supports configurable endpoints to accommodate different Mulesoft regions and deployment environments including US Commercial Cloud, EU Cloud, Government Cloud, and on-premises deployments. It uses multi-stream support so you can connect to multiple Mulesoft environments simultaneously. Configure the connector with your Anypoint Platform credentials, environment ID, and optional application name filter to begin collecting data into the MulesoftCloudhubAlerts table. Refer to [Mulesoft CloudHub API documentation](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/) for more information about the CloudHub APIs.

dataTypes.name: MulesoftCloudhubAlerts
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
MulesoftCloudhubAlerts
| where TimeGenerated > ago(6h)
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Mulesoft CloudHub API access
customs.description: **Mulesoft CloudHub API** access with read permissions is required.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Mulesoft CloudHub Connections
instructionSteps.description: Manage connections to your Mulesoft CloudHub environments

parameters.content:
## Prerequisites

Before configuring this connector, ensure you have:

1. **Mulesoft Anypoint Platform Account** with appropriate permissions
2. **Environment Access** to the CloudHub environment you want to monitor
3. **API Access Credentials** (username and password)
4. **Environment ID** from your Mulesoft CloudHub console

### Finding Your Environment ID

1. Log in to [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/)
2. Navigate to **Access Management** > **Environments**
3. Select your target environment
4. Copy the Environment ID from the URL or environment details

parameters.text: This connector requires read access to the CloudHub API. Contact your Mulesoft administrator if you don't have the necessary permissions.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Add Mulesoft CloudHub Connection
parameters.subtitle: Configure a connection to a Mulesoft CloudHub environment

instructionSteps.title: Base Configuration
instructionSteps.description: Configure your Mulesoft CloudHub API connection

parameters.text: Enter a unique alias to identify this connection. Use descriptive names like 'US-Production', 'EU-Staging', etc.

instructionSteps.title: Environment Configuration
instructionSteps.description: Configure the CloudHub environment to monitor

parameters.label: Environment ID
parameters.placeholder: CloudHub Environment ID (found in Anypoint Platform)
parameters.name: environmentId

parameters.label: Application Name Filter
parameters.placeholder: Optional: Filter alerts for a specific application
parameters.name: applicationName

parameters.text: Leave the application name filter empty to collect alerts for all applications in the environment.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Monitor and Validate
instructionSteps.description: Monitor data ingestion and validate connectivity

parameters.content:
### Post-Connection Steps

1. **Monitor Connection Status**: Check the connector status in the Data connectors page
2. **Validate Data Flow**: Use the sample queries provided to verify data is being collected
3. **Review Logs**: Check Azure Monitor logs for any connection or authentication issues

### Data Collection Schedule
- **Alerts**: Polled every 5 minutes from `/v2/alerts` endpoint

### Expected Timeline
- **Initial Data**: Allow 10-15 minutes for initial data to appear
- **Alerts Data**: Available in the `MulesoftCloudhubAlerts` table

### Troubleshooting
- **Authentication errors**: Verify credentials and environment access
- **No data**: Ensure alerts exist in the specified environment
- **Partial data**: Check environment ID and application filter settings

parameters.text: **Sample Query**: `MulesoftCloudhubAlerts | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) | summarize count() by Severity`
